item.title:
OIL COULD JUMP 50% IF MIDEAST WAR SPREADS
Edward Morse warns that crude prices could rise 50% if Middle East fighting widens. He notes the U.S. lags in feeling the impact, but gasoline could hit $4 a gallon as oil surges.
item.description:
OIL COULD JUMP 50% IF MIDEAST WAR SPREADS

Edward Morse warns that crude prices could rise 50% if Middle East fighting widens. He notes the U.S. lags in feeling the impact, but gasoline could hit $4 a gallon as oil surges.

item.title: US missile hit military base near Iran school, video analysis shows
item.description: A US Tomahawk missile hit a military base near a primary school in southern Iran where Iranian authorities said 168 people were killed, expert video analysis shows.

item.title: G7 ‘stands ready’ to release emergency oil reserves
item.description: Finance ministers pledge ‘necessary measures’ to tackle surging crude prices but stop short of immediate deal

item.title:
TRUMP SAYS NO IRAN GROUND MISSION—AND HE’S “NOT HAPPY” WITH NEW LEADER
Donald Trump told The Post that the U.S. is “nowhere near” deploying troops into Iran to secure the nuclear site at Isfahan, dismissing active plans for a ground mission despite media reports of internal discussions.
Trump also said he is “not happy” with the recent appointment of Mojtaba Khamenei as Iran’s new supreme leader after the death of his father, calling him unacceptable and declining to detail how he plans to respond.
He reiterated that no decision has been made on U.S. troop deployment in Iran and kept his strategy close to the vest amid escalating geopolitical tensions.
item.description:
TRUMP SAYS NO IRAN GROUND MISSION—AND HE’S “NOT HAPPY” WITH NEW LEADER

Donald Trump told The Post that the U.S. is “nowhere near” deploying troops into Iran to secure the nuclear site at Isfahan, dismissing active plans for a ground mission despite media reports of internal discussions.

Trump also said he is “not happy” with the recent appointment of Mojtaba Khamenei as Iran’s new supreme leader after the death of his father, calling him unacceptable and declining to detail how he plans to respond.

He reiterated that no decision has been made on U.S. troop deployment in Iran and kept his strategy close to the vest amid escalating geopolitical tensions.

item.title: How the Iran war may affect your bills and finances
item.description: The conflict in the Middle East could raise the cost of petrol, household energy bills and even food.

item.title: IRAN WARNS $200 A BARREL OIL IF STRIKES ON ITS INFRASTRUCTURE DON'T STOP -- WSJ
item.description: IRAN WARNS $200 A BARREL OIL IF STRIKES ON ITS INFRASTRUCTURE DON'T STOP -- WSJ

item.title:
IRAN WARNS OF $200 OIL
An Iranian military spokesperson said oil could hit $200 a barrel if U.S. and Israeli strikes on its energy infrastructure continue.
Recent attacks, including Israel hitting Tehran refinery storage tanks, forced Iran to cut fuel allowances. Since the conflict began, Iran and its proxies have targeted oil facilities in Saudi Arabia, Iraq, and Kuwait, reducing regional output.
item.description:
IRAN WARNS OF $200 OIL

An Iranian military spokesperson said oil could hit $200 a barrel if U.S. and Israeli strikes on its energy infrastructure continue.

Recent attacks, including Israel hitting Tehran refinery storage tanks, forced Iran to cut fuel allowances. Since the conflict began, Iran and its proxies have targeted oil facilities in Saudi Arabia, Iraq, and Kuwait, reducing regional output.

item.description: FRANCE SAYS G-7 NOT THERE YET ON RELEASING OIL STOCKPILES

item.title: Five Iranian footballers 'in Australian safe house' after Asian Cup protest
item.description: Concern has grown for team after one critic called them 'wartime traitors' for failing to salute during the Iranian anthem.

item.description: LESCURE: READY TO USE STRATEGIC OIL RESERVE TO STABILIZE MARKET

item.title:
HORMUZ CLOSURE COULD PUSH OIL TO $150
Macquarie warns that if the Strait of Hormuz stays closed for weeks, Brent crude could surge to $150+ due to production shut-ins and limited transit.
In afternoon trading, Brent rose 10% to $102.21 and WTI gained 8.9% to $89.50.
item.description:
HORMUZ CLOSURE COULD PUSH OIL TO $150

Macquarie warns that if the Strait of Hormuz stays closed for weeks, Brent crude could surge to $150+ due to production shut-ins and limited transit.

In afternoon trading, Brent rose 10% to $102.21 and WTI gained 8.9% to $89.50.

item.title:
FEBRUARY CPI SEEN NOT SHIFTING FED POLICY
BofA says February CPI is unlikely to affect near-term Fed decisions, with headline inflation expected +0.3% MoM and core CPI +0.2%, reflecting contained consumer prices.
item.description:
FEBRUARY CPI SEEN NOT SHIFTING FED POLICY

BofA says February CPI is unlikely to affect near-term Fed decisions, with headline inflation expected +0.3% MoM and core CPI +0.2%, reflecting contained consumer prices.

item.title:
IRAN WAR THREATENS GLOBAL FERTILIZER SUPPLIES
The Iran conflict risks disrupting fertilizer production critical to global food, as the Middle East hosts major plants and raw material exporters.
About 25–35% of these raw materials pass through the Strait of Hormuz, now effectively closed. Iran, the fourth-largest urea exporter, and Saudi Arabia face supply hits, pushing Egyptian urea prices up 33%. Sulphur prices have also surged, with nearly half of global exports from the region.
Attacks on energy infrastructure, like Qatar’s gas cuts, could further threaten fertilizer supply, which CEO Svein Tore Holsether says underpins half of global food production.
item.description:
IRAN WAR THREATENS GLOBAL FERTILIZER SUPPLIES

The Iran conflict risks disrupting fertilizer production critical to global food, as the Middle East hosts major plants and raw material exporters.

About 25–35% of these raw materials pass through the Strait of Hormuz, now effectively closed. Iran, the fourth-largest urea exporter, and Saudi Arabia face supply hits, pushing Egyptian urea prices up 33%. Sulphur prices have also surged, with nearly half of global exports from the region.

Attacks on energy infrastructure, like Qatar’s gas cuts, could further threaten fertilizer supply, which CEO Svein Tore Holsether says underpins half of global food production.

item.description: American consumers will feel pain at the petrol pump, but US is a net energy exporter unlike its European allies

item.title: 🚨 IRAQ'S KIRKUK CRUDE OIL FLOWS TO TURKEY'S CEYHAN PORT STOPPED ON MARCH 9 - SHIPPING SOURCE
item.description: 🚨 IRAQ'S KIRKUK CRUDE OIL FLOWS TO TURKEY'S CEYHAN PORT STOPPED ON MARCH 9 - SHIPPING SOURCE

item.title:
STRAIT OF HORMUZ CRISIS HITS GLOBAL ENERGY
The near-closure of the Strait of Hormuz—a key route for 25% of seaborne oil and 20% of LNG—has disrupted tanker traffic and pushed crude above $100, the first time since 2022.
Backups in the Persian Gulf, filling storage, and production cuts by Saudi Arabia signal the conflict is shifting from military to economic shock, risking slower growth and renewed inflation pressures worldwide.
item.description:
STRAIT OF HORMUZ CRISIS HITS GLOBAL ENERGY

The near-closure of the Strait of Hormuz—a key route for 25% of seaborne oil and 20% of LNG—has disrupted tanker traffic and pushed crude above $100, the first time since 2022.

Backups in the Persian Gulf, filling storage, and production cuts by Saudi Arabia signal the conflict is shifting from military to economic shock, risking slower growth and renewed inflation pressures worldwide.

item.title: Emergency oil reserves could be tapped - but few would bet on prices falling
item.description: A big intervention is being discussed in the oil markets, but as yet, we do not know how big the problem will be.

item.title:
JPMORGAN WAR RISKS SIGNAL 10% S&P 500 DROP
Andrew Tyler warns that the S&P 500 could fall 10% from its peak as the Iran conflict pushes oil above $100 and energy supply risks rise.
Investors are underprepared, with positioning largely neutral, while energy stocks were recently sold on the expectation of de-escalation. Tyler notes the tactical bearish call would end if the conflict is resolved, as macro fundamentals remain supportive.
item.description:
JPMORGAN WAR RISKS SIGNAL 10% S&P 500 DROP

Andrew Tyler warns that the S&P 500 could fall 10% from its peak as the Iran conflict pushes oil above $100 and energy supply risks rise.

Investors are underprepared, with positioning largely neutral, while energy stocks were recently sold on the expectation of de-escalation. Tyler notes the tactical bearish call would end if the conflict is resolved, as macro fundamentals remain supportive.

item.title:
HIGH OIL PRICES THREATEN GROWTH
Phoebe White warns that rising oil amid the Middle East conflict may boost inflation expectations, but sustained higher prices could suppress demand, hurt growth, and lower inflation, undermining the ongoing business sentiment recovery.
item.description:
HIGH OIL PRICES THREATEN GROWTH

Phoebe White warns that rising oil amid the Middle East conflict may boost inflation expectations, but sustained higher prices could suppress demand, hurt growth, and lower inflation, undermining the ongoing business sentiment recovery.

item.title:
IRAN WAR STRATEGY: MAKE IT PAINFUL FOR THE U.S.
Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf warned on X that Tehran aims to make the war so costly the U.S. must back off. He said the conflict could keep oil above $100 and cause long-lasting global economic disruption.
item.description:

item.title: Passer-by tried to tackle vape shop blaze that led to inferno
item.description: Lamin Kongira told BBC Scotland News he had been walking past when a shopkeeper ran out saying "fire, fire".

item.title:
IRAN WAR LIFTS MARKET MELTDOWN RISK
Ed Yardeni has raised the chance of a U.S. stock market meltdown to 35%, up from 20%, as the Iran war pushes oil above $100 and clouds the outlook.
He also cut the odds of a “melt-up” rally to 5%, citing rising energy costs and reduced expectations for rate cuts from the Federal Reserve.
The S&P 500 has so far held up better than global markets, while the Bloomberg Dollar Spot Index has climbed as investors seek safety.
Despite the risks, Yardeni still assigns a 60% probability to his “Roaring 2020s” growth scenario.
item.description:
IRAN WAR LIFTS MARKET MELTDOWN RISK

Ed Yardeni has raised the chance of a U.S. stock market meltdown to 35%, up from 20%, as the Iran war pushes oil above $100 and clouds the outlook.

He also cut the odds of a “melt-up” rally to 5%, citing rising energy costs and reduced expectations for rate cuts from the Federal Reserve.

The S&P 500 has so far held up better than global markets, while the Bloomberg Dollar Spot Index has climbed as investors seek safety.

Despite the risks, Yardeni still assigns a 60% probability to his “Roaring 2020s” growth scenario.

item.title: FRENCH PRESIDENT MACRON: WHEN CYPRUS IS ATTACKED, THEN EUROPE IS ATTACKED
item.description: FRENCH PRESIDENT MACRON: WHEN CYPRUS IS ATTACKED, THEN EUROPE IS ATTACKED

item.description: CME GROUP ENERGY COMPLEX REACHES ALL-TIME DAILY VOLUME RECORD OF 8.3 MILLION CONTRACTS

item.title:
LATEST FROM THE IRAN CONFLICT:
🔸Oil spikes: Brent Crude jumped to $119.50 before easing to around $102 after reports G7 finance ministers may discuss a coordinated oil reserve release.
🔸NATO intercept: NATO shot down an Iranian ballistic missile headed for Turkey, raising the risk of wider involvement.
🔸Hormuz disruption: With the Strait of Hormuz nearly shut, Gulf producers face storage shortages. Saudi Arabia has begun cutting output.
🔸Trump reaction: Donald Trump said higher oil prices are a “small price” for security and is set to address House Republicans.
🔸New Iranian leader: Mojtaba Khamenei has replaced his father as supreme leader after the U.S.-Israeli strikes and is expected to maintain a hardline stance.
item.description:
LATEST FROM THE IRAN CONFLICT:

🔸Oil spikes: Brent Crude jumped to $119.50 before easing to around $102 after reports G7 finance ministers may discuss a coordinated oil reserve release.

🔸NATO intercept: NATO shot down an Iranian ballistic missile headed for Turkey, raising the risk of wider involvement.

🔸Hormuz disruption: With the Strait of Hormuz nearly shut, Gulf producers face storage shortages. Saudi Arabia has begun cutting output.

🔸Trump reaction: Donald Trump said higher oil prices are a “small price” for security and is set to address House Republicans.

🔸New Iranian leader: Mojtaba Khamenei has replaced his father as supreme leader after the U.S.-Israeli strikes and is expected to maintain a hardline stance.

item.title:
OIL SURGE CLOUDS FED RATE CUT OUTLOOK
Rising oil prices pose the biggest risk to the inflation outlook, says Jonathan Miller of Barclays.
The bank still expects the Federal Reserve to deliver two 25bp rate cuts in June and December, but warns oil is a key wildcard. A 10% oil rise could lift inflation by about 0.2 percentage points within months.
Barclays now sees December 2026 CPI at 2.7%, while recent data still points to a stable labor market and gradually slowing consumer spending.
item.description:
OIL SURGE CLOUDS FED RATE CUT OUTLOOK

Rising oil prices pose the biggest risk to the inflation outlook, says Jonathan Miller of Barclays.

The bank still expects the Federal Reserve to deliver two 25bp rate cuts in June and December, but warns oil is a key wildcard. A 10% oil rise could lift inflation by about 0.2 percentage points within months.

Barclays now sees December 2026 CPI at 2.7%, while recent data still points to a stable labor market and gradually slowing consumer spending.

item.title:
S&P 500 CORRECTION NEARING END
Michael Wilson of Morgan Stanley says the S&P 500 is likely closer to the end than the start of its rolling correction.
He remains constructive over the next 6–12 months, expecting earnings growth and market breadth to improve. However, moves in oil and the U.S. dollar will determine how long volatility lasts.
Morgan Stanley says near-term weakness could create buying opportunities, especially in Financials, Industrials, Consumer Discretionary and small caps, though risks remain if oil stays above $100 or the Federal Reserve delays rate cuts.
item.description:
S&P 500 CORRECTION NEARING END

Michael Wilson of Morgan Stanley says the S&P 500 is likely closer to the end than the start of its rolling correction.

He remains constructive over the next 6–12 months, expecting earnings growth and market breadth to improve. However, moves in oil and the U.S. dollar will determine how long volatility lasts.

Morgan Stanley says near-term weakness could create buying opportunities, especially in Financials, Industrials, Consumer Discretionary and small caps, though risks remain if oil stays above $100 or the Federal Reserve delays rate cuts.

item.title:
MARKETS MAY NEED MORE PAIN BEFORE REBOUND
Mislav Matejka of JPMorgan warns equities could face more short-term weakness before stabilizing, as geopolitical tensions drive a brief wave of risk reduction.
He expects the episode to last days or weeks, not months, meaning the selloff could ultimately create a buying opportunity once positioning clears and oversold areas emerge.
Near-term risks remain, especially from oil and bonds. Oil could spike further in the short term—though the move so far is smaller than during the Russia–Ukraine War—while U.S. gasoline prices have already jumped 10–15%.
Matejka says markets could find a bottom this or next week, with potential opportunities in Industrials, semiconductors, consumer discretionary, emerging markets and the eurozone. Oversold hyperscalers and AI laggards may also see a short-term rebound.
item.description:
MARKETS MAY NEED MORE PAIN BEFORE REBOUND

Mislav Matejka of JPMorgan warns equities could face more short-term weakness before stabilizing, as geopolitical tensions drive a brief wave of risk reduction.

He expects the episode to last days or weeks, not months, meaning the selloff could ultimately create a buying opportunity once positioning clears and oversold areas emerge.

Near-term risks remain, especially from oil and bonds. Oil could spike further in the short term—though the move so far is smaller than during the Russia–Ukraine War—while U.S. gasoline prices have already jumped 10–15%.

Matejka says markets could find a bottom this or next week, with potential opportunities in Industrials, semiconductors, consumer discretionary, emerging markets and the eurozone. Oversold hyperscalers and AI laggards may also see a short-term rebound.

item.title:
OIL SPIKE MAY REVERSE FAST
Oil prices could fall as sharply as they rose, warns Derek Podhaizer of Piper Sandler.
Despite a ~40% jump in WTI last week, U.S. oilfield services stocks barely moved. Halliburton fell about 5%, roughly in line with the VanEck Oil Services ETF (OIH).
Podhaizer says the muted reaction reflects already strong year-to-date gains and skepticism that producers will quickly boost drilling due to capital discipline.
If the conflict eases soon, oil could drop as quickly as it spiked, creating downside risk for service stocks. Middle East-exposed names, including National Energy Services Reunited and SLB, have already fallen amid risks tied to disruptions around the Strait of Hormuz.
item.description:
OIL SPIKE MAY REVERSE FAST

Oil prices could fall as sharply as they rose, warns Derek Podhaizer of Piper Sandler.

Despite a ~40% jump in WTI last week, U.S. oilfield services stocks barely moved. Halliburton fell about 5%, roughly in line with the VanEck Oil Services ETF (OIH).

Podhaizer says the muted reaction reflects already strong year-to-date gains and skepticism that producers will quickly boost drilling due to capital discipline.

If the conflict eases soon, oil could drop as quickly as it spiked, creating downside risk for service stocks. Middle East-exposed names, including National Energy Services Reunited and SLB, have already fallen amid risks tied to disruptions around the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Watch: Why the Iran war is pushing up oil prices
item.description: As turmoil continues in the Middle East, the price of crude oil rose to more than $100 a barrel on Monday.
